name: Boundary Refactor Wave O — Client/Browser Authority Leaks
outcome_goal: Client-side authority logic in surfaces/app/src/build/BuildWorkspace.tsx, surfaces/app/src/builder/intentParser.ts, surfaces/app/src/builder/pipelineConvert.ts, surfaces/app/src/builder/queueSpecExport.ts, surfaces/app/src/builder/taskRouting.ts, surfaces/app/src/canvas/commitmentMapShared.ts, surfaces/app/src/dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx moves to server-side API endpoints and authority modules; the browser becomes a thin renderer over server truth; no compilation, routing, or persistence policy lives in the client.
anti_requirements: Do NOT remove client UX — interactive behaviour stays, just server-authoritative
Do NOT change the public API contracts of existing server endpoints without tests
Do NOT ship partial migrations — each file moves in one coherent commit with tests

jobs.label: wave_o_plan
jobs.outcome_goal: A written plan names every repository/authority method, every migration, and every callsite
jobs.prompt:
You are planning Wave O — Client/Browser Authority Leaks. Do not write code yet.

Linked bugs:
- BUG-D0633FD644AA — surfaces/app/src/build/BuildWorkspace.tsx:866-964: client control plane
- BUG-5001FA81C604 — surfaces/app/src/dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx: freshness + commit eligibility
- BUG-73E7EB966B45 — surfaces/app/src/canvas/commitmentMapShared.ts:131-204: fallback subflow
- BUG-C6B368E8C549 — surfaces/app/src/builder/queueSpecExport.ts:123-175: client spec assembly
- BUG-DD9D1DF37451 — surfaces/app/src/builder/taskRouting.ts:21-101: client task authority
- BUG-C66E4C997F15 — surfaces/app/src/builder/intentParser.ts:521-1091: client compilation
- BUG-87242329957C — surfaces/app/src/builder/pipelineConvert.ts:21-145: client topology

Sources with leaked authority:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/build/BuildWorkspace.tsx
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/intentParser.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/pipelineConvert.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/queueSpecExport.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/taskRouting.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/canvas/commitmentMapShared.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx

Target destinations:
- server-side API endpoints under surfaces/api/
- authority modules owning compilation / routing / freshness / spec assembly

Read each source file fully and produce a written plan naming:
1. Every method signature for every new/extended repository or authority module
2. Which tables already have migrations and which need a new migration file (name the number)
3. Every callsite that will need follow-up changes
4. The exact grep-based regression check each downstream job must pass

Reference idiom: storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py, storage/postgres/workflow_runtime_repository.py, storage/postgres/validators.py.

jobs.label: wave_o_migrations
jobs.outcome_goal: No CREATE TABLE remains in source files; any missing table has a migration
jobs.prompt:
For Wave O, audit every source file for inline CREATE TABLE or CREATE INDEX statements. For each one, either confirm it has a matching migration under Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/ or add a new migration with the next available number. Match columns and constraints exactly — this is a move, not a redesign. Do NOT edit the source files yet.

Sources:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/build/BuildWorkspace.tsx
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/intentParser.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/pipelineConvert.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/queueSpecExport.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/taskRouting.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/canvas/commitmentMapShared.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available; ensure_postgres_available()'

jobs.label: wave_o_create_repositories
jobs.outcome_goal: Every needed repository/authority class exists with typed methods and package exports
jobs.prompt:
Create the repository/authority modules named in the Wave O plan. Mirror the idiom from storage/postgres/memory_graph_repository.py: class Postgres<Domain>Repository, __init__(conn), explicit method names, input validation via storage.postgres.validators.

Cover every INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE currently in these source files:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/build/BuildWorkspace.tsx
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/intentParser.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/pipelineConvert.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/queueSpecExport.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/taskRouting.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/canvas/commitmentMapShared.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx

Target files to create:
- server-side API endpoints under surfaces/api/
- authority modules owning compilation / routing / freshness / spec assembly

Export new classes from Code&DBs/Workflow/storage/postgres/__init__.py so downstream packages can import them. Do NOT edit the source files yet — the thinning step happens next.
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && PYTHONPATH='Code&DBs/Workflow' /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 -c 'from storage.postgres import ensure_postgres_available; ensure_postgres_available()'

jobs.label: wave_o_thin_sources
jobs.description: Thin the source files to delegate through repositories
jobs.outcome_goal: No SQL or CREATE TABLE remains in any source file; public APIs unchanged
jobs.prompt:
Rewrite each source file to delegate every write to the repositories/authority modules created in the previous job. Keep public function and class signatures unchanged. Remove every SQL string and every CREATE TABLE.

Sources to thin:
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/build/BuildWorkspace.tsx
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/intentParser.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/pipelineConvert.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/queueSpecExport.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/builder/taskRouting.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/canvas/commitmentMapShared.ts
- Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx

For each source file, verify with:
```
! grep -E 'INSERT |UPDATE |DELETE FROM|CREATE TABLE' <file>
python3 -m py_compile <file>
```

jobs.label: wave_o_tests
jobs.description: Prove the migration with tests and regression assertions
jobs.outcome_goal: Round-trip tests + grep regression tests pass; existing suites stay green
jobs.prompt:
Add or extend tests for Wave O.

Steps:
1. Run existing tests under Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/ that exercise the source files.
2. For each new repository or authority module, add at least one round-trip test.
3. Add a single regression test file that opens each source file as text and asserts it contains no 'INSERT ', 'UPDATE ', 'DELETE FROM', or 'CREATE TABLE' substrings.
4. Run the relevant test subset.

jobs.label: wave_o_review
jobs.description: Independent review of the wave rewrite
jobs.outcome_goal: Any remaining leaks, stale DDL, or missed callsites are called out in writing
jobs.prompt:
Review the Wave O rewrite as a code review.

Prioritize findings on:
- any residual SQL strings or CREATE TABLE in the source files
- callsites that relied on exception types/return shapes that changed
- lost observability or silenced errors in the new repositories/authority modules
- missed or weak tests around upsert semantics and error paths
- whether new classes are exported from storage/postgres/__init__.py

Findings first. If no findings remain, say so explicitly.
